MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Nicole E. Gage, Esq., of TUCKER ELLIS & WEST (415-617-2409), appearing for defendant / moving party Rockwell Automation, Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Fabian Carcallas, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 3 (negligence/strict liability and punitive damages) and granted as to issue 2 (false representation). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Carcallas was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Carcallas' deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar on this issue. There are triable issues whether defendant acted in conscious disregard for the safety of persons who were exposed to its asbestos-containing products. On plaintiffs' claim for false representation, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant ever made any representation. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Scott Hughes, CSR # 12365 = (220/HEK)

Off Calendar Jan-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties ESTELA CARCALLAS, as wrongful death heir, and as SSI to FABIAN CARCALLAS, deceased; and FABIAN CACALLAS III, OLIVER CARCALLAS, as legal heirs of FABIAN CARCALLAS, deceased
Darin A. Leviloff, Esq. of HOWARD ROME MARTIN & RIDLEY (650-365-7715, X211), appearing for defendant / opposing party Eaton Electrical, Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' motion to compel defendant Eaton Electrical, Inc. to produce its custodian of records and person(s) most knowledgeable for deposition, and to produce documents at deposition. No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to compel is granted. Eaton is required to produce a witness for deposition. A declaration, interrogatory responses and/or prior deposition testimony is insufficient. If Eaton selects Mr. Bonness, then Mr. Bonness may be in Milwaukee at the time of his deposition and plaintiff and other parties may participate by telephone, videoconferencing or other means. If Eaton selects someone other than Mr. Bonness, the parties are ordered to meet and confer about the location of the deposition. The parties are required to meet and confer about the subject areas of the deposition to limit the deposition to those areas that are relevant and likely to lead to relevant evidence in this case. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Not reported
